channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US envoy says Israel-Hezbollah truce is 'within our grasp' as Gaza food crisis worsens after looting
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A United States envoy said an agreement to end the Israel-Hezbollah war is “within our grasp” after talks in Lebanon on Tuesday. However, there was no such optimism in the Gaza Strip, where the looting of nearly 100 aid trucks by armed men worsened an already severe food crisis.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gabby Petito's family asks court to overturn police immunity in their civil case against Moab
item.description: The family of murder victim Gabby Petito is asking a Utah court to end the state's immunity for police officers as they attempt to move forward with a $50 million lawsuit against the Moab Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Scott Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jeffries: Republicans don't have a mandate
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Tuesday rejected the idea that voters delivered an Election Day mandate to Donald Trump and the Republicans, arguing that the GOP’s razor-thin majority in the House means that nothing will get done in Washington without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans fume as GOP absences help Democrats move judicial nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans aired frustrations Tuesday after Vice President-elect Vance and other party members skipped votes Monday, greasing the skids for Democratic-backed judicial nominees to be greenlighted as part of a final push to fill the bench with lifetime appointees before President-elect Trump t
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trial starts for New Hampshire youth center worker accused of holding down boy while he was raped
item.description: MANCHESTER, N.H. (AP) — A man who says he was brutally raped as a teenager by workers at a New Hampshire youth center testified Tuesday that he couldn't report the attack at the time because the perpetrators were responsible for his welfare. “They just did that to you.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: California governor will not make clemency decision for Menendez brothers until new DA reviews case
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says he won’t make a clemency decision on the murder convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez until newly elected Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman reviews the case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 51-year-old man is charged with murder after 3 are stabbed in New York City rampage
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man has been charged with three counts of first-degree murder in the fatal stabbings of three people in a string of random attacks across Manhattan, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Survivors of LGBTQ+ club shooting say the deaths and trauma could've been avoided in lawsuits
item.description: Victims and family of those killed in the mass shooting at an LGBTQ+ nightclub in Colorado Springs in 2022 spoke Tuesday after filing lawsuits against the El Paso County commissioners and former sheriff.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Another rare 'doomsday fish' discovered off Southern California coast for the third time this year
item.description: Another oarfish has made its way from the deep sea to the coast of San Diego County in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KTLA 5

item.title: Senate report claims Biden failures in protecting migrant children
item.description: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy launched a formal investigation more than a year ago into unaccompanied migrant kids.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Carcass of endangered fin whale washes up near Alaska's largest city
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An endangered fin whale that washed up near a coastal trail in Alaska's largest city has attracted curious onlookers while biologists seek answers as to what caused the animal's death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Survivor of deadly trip across Canada-US border expected to testify in human smuggling trial
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — An Indian migrant who survived a deadly trek across the Canada-U.S. border in blizzard conditions is expected to testify Tuesday in the federal trial of two men accused of taking part in a sprawling human smuggling scheme that ultimately killed a family of four.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gov Newsom announces decision in Menendez brothers case
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says he will respect the will of the voters after they replaced the Los Angeles district attorney who made a last-minute push in case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The White House's Christmas tree is a symbol of resilience for hurricane-hit North Carolina farms
item.description: The Cartner family had known since last year that one of their farm’s Christmas trees would be headed to the White House this winter.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling because of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump left office in early 2021, the pandemic was at its height. Now he returns as American children and schools are struggling to overcome its lingering impact.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Second-grader 'traumatized' after being hung in school bathroom during 'horseplaying' incident: report
item.description: A 7-year-old Maryland student is recovering after reportedly being found hanging in a school bathroom last week, in an incident officials say was a result of "horseplaying.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oxford families push for subpoenas 3 years after Ethan Crumbley killed 4 in school shooting
item.description: Ethan Crumbley injured seven and shot dead four on Nov. 30, 2021. The victims' parents gathered on Monday to demand accountability from Oxford High School staff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Speaker Johnson's 'epic' weekend with Trump shows strengths and limits of his power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was House Speaker Mike Johnson walking behind President-elect Donald Trump's entourage into Saturday night's UFC fight at Madison Square Garden, his stature overcome by the enormity of the scene around him. And Johnson mugging with musicians Kid Rock and Jelly Roll.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MTA board approves Democrat Gov. Hochul’s massive NYC congestion pricing toll reboot
item.description: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority approved a congestion pricing program pitched by New York Gov. Kathy Hochul to charge as much as $15 for drivers to enter New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rapper NBA YoungBoy pleads guilty in Utah prescription drug fraud ring case
item.description: Famed rapper NBA YoungBoy entered a plea deal with Utah prosecutors over his involvement in a large-scale prescription drug fraud ring.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KRON4

item.title: Suspect in custody after 'unprovoked' New York City stabbing spree leaves 3 people dead: police
item.description: Two men were killed and a woman left fighting for her life after a homeless New York City man allegedly committed a series of random stabbings, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police seek witnesses, cell phone video after gunfire during a parade in New Orleans kills 2
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Deadly shootings at a signature New Orleans cultural event — a neighborhood “second-line” parade — left authorities pleading with witnesses to come forward with information or cell phone video while seeking to reassure the public that the weekend bloodshed was an aberration in a c
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How a 1966 wedding vinyl ended up at a Texas thrift store — and found its way back home
item.description: A 1966 wedding record is finally making its way back home after accidentally being donated to an Austin, Texas, thrift store.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Thompson, Fox 5

item.title: Advocates urge Biden to dismantle migrant detention before Trump takes office
item.description: Nearly 200 migrant advocacy groups have asked President Joe Biden to dismantle U.S. immigration detention facilities before President-Elect Donald Trump takes office, and to give humanitarian parole to some migrants currently detained.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Steve Bannon's trial in a border wall charity scheme case delayed until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon 's border wall fundraising trial was postponed to February as prosecutors disclosed Monday that the former Trump White House strategist himself once suggested the nonprofit venture was “a scam.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: India has overtaken China as the top source of international students for U.S. colleges, according to a report from Open Doors.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Man attacked by owls twice in less than a week
item.description: Owls aren't notoriously menacing creatures, but those living in the Old Shell Road area might not agree.
item.dc:creator: Asher Redd, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Neo-Nazi march in Ohio draws rebuke from Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden joined the chorus of state and city officials who are condemning a weekend neo-Nazi march in Columbus, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, KRON4

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Student protesters face potential Trump crackdown
item.description: Those on campus have already seen stricter rules around demonstrations this academic year, but President-elect Trump, as a candidate, has threatened protesters with everything from military action to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These are the worst passwords you can have in 2024
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Boeing issues layoff notices to 400-plus workers as it begins drastic cuts
item.description: Boeing has delivered layoff notices to more than 400 members of its professional aerospace labor union.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Draymond Green's foul against Zach Edey is upgraded by the NBA to a flagrant 1
item.description: Golden State forward Draymond Green's foul against Memphis' Zach Edey in the third quarter of the Warriors' victory over the Grizzlies has been upgraded to a category-one flagrant, the NBA said Saturday after reviewing the play.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Inside one of the most consequential UFO encounters of all time: the 'tic tac' incident
item.description: Pentagon officials did their best to downplay the incident, and the US Navy didn't bother to investigate, but the "Tic Tac" incident did lead to a significant investigation even if the public wasn't made aware until years later.
item.dc:creator: George Knapp, WGN 9
